info.name: Restful-Booker
info.description: Test suite for the Restful Booker API covering authentication and booking management. Includes happy path, negative cases, edge cases, security, and performance scenarios.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Tests for the POST /auth endpoint that issues access tokens.

item.name: 00 - Setup
item.description: Generates the auth token stored in the environment for use by all authenticated requests.

item.name: Generate Token
item.description: Obtains a valid auth token using demo credentials. Stores the token as an environment variable.

item.name: 01 - Happy Path
item.description: Verifies that valid credentials return a well-formed token with the correct response shape.

item.name: Login valid
item.description: Expects status 200, a non-empty token string, correct Content-Type, and response time under 500ms.

item.name: 02 - Negative Cases
item.description: Verifies the API rejects invalid credentials with the expected error response.

item.name: Wrong password
item.description: Sends a valid username with an incorrect password. Expects the API to return a 'Bad credentials' reason.

item.name: Wrong username
item.description: Sends a non-existent username. Expects the API to return a 'Bad credentials' reason.

item.name: Empty body
item.description: Sends a request with no body. Expects the API to reject it without issuing a token.

item.name: 03 - Security
item.description: Verifies the API does not execute injected payloads passed as credential values.

item.name: SQL Injection attempt
item.description: Sends a SQL injection string as the username. Expects no server error and no token in the response.

exec: pm.test("SQL Injection did not return a token", () => {

item.name: XSS attempt
item.description: Sends an XSS payload as the username. Expects no server error and no token in the response.

exec: pm.test("XSS attempt did not return a token", () => {

item.name: 04 - Performance
item.description: Spot-checks API response time to catch latency regressions.

item.name: Get all bookings response time
item.description: Asserts GET /booking responds in under 500ms as a baseline performance check.

item.name: Booking
item.description: Tests for the /booking endpoint covering CRUD operations on booking resources.

item.name: 00 - Setup
item.description: Creates the baseline booking used by all happy path and negative tests in this module.

item.name: Create Booking
item.description: Creates a booking for Brandon Sanderson and stores the booking_id and all field values as collection variables for downstream tests.

exec: pm.test("Booking created with an id", function () {
pm.test("Response contains booking with all sent fields", function () {

item.name: 01 - Happy Path
item.description: Verifies all supported read and write operations against a valid booking.

item.name: Get all bookings
item.description: Expects a non-empty array of booking IDs from GET /booking.

item.name: Get booking by id
item.description: Fetches the booking created in Setup and validates the full response schema and all field values.

exec: pm.test("Booking data matches setup values", function () {

item.name: Get by name
item.description: Filters bookings by firstname and lastname. Expects the Setup booking to appear in the results.

exec: pm.test("At least one result exists", function () {
pm.test("Created booking id exists in response", function () {

item.name: Get by check dates
item.description: Filters bookings by checkin and checkout query params. Verifies the endpoint accepts date filters.

exec: // Known limitation: Restful-Booker's date filter is unreliable —
// it does not consistently return bookings whose checkin/checkout
// match the filter values. We verify the endpoint responds correctly
// and returns the expected shape, but skip the ID inclusion check.

item.name: Update booking
item.description: Replaces the entire booking record via PUT. Verifies all updated fields are persisted correctly.

item.name: Partial update
item.description: Updates only firstname via PATCH. Verifies the change is applied without affecting other fields.

item.name: Delete booking
item.description: Deletes the Setup booking. Expects 201 as the API's delete confirmation status.

item.name: Get booking after delete
item.description: Fetches the deleted booking. Expects 404 to confirm the record no longer exists.

exec: pm.test("Status code is 404 - booking no longer exists", function () {

item.name: 02 - Negative Cases
item.description: Verifies the API returns appropriate errors for invalid inputs and unauthorized requests.

item.name: Get booking - invalid ID
item.description: Requests a booking with a non-existent numeric ID. Expects 404.

item.name: Create booking - missing bookingdates
item.description: Submits a booking without the required bookingdates field. Expects a 4xx error response.

exec: pm.test("Status code is 500 - missing required fields", function () {

item.name: Update booking - no auth
item.description: Attempts a full update without providing an auth token. Expects 403 Forbidden.

item.name: Delete booking - no auth
item.description: Attempts to delete a booking without an auth token. Expects 403 Forbidden.

item.name: 03 - Edge Cases
item.description: Tests boundary values and unusual inputs to verify the API handles them gracefully.

item.name: Create booking - totalprice zero
item.description: Verifies the API accepts 0 as a valid totalprice. Stores the booking ID for cleanup.

item.name: Create booking - special characters in name
item.description: Verifies the API accepts Unicode and special characters (José García-López) in name fields. Stores the booking ID for cleanup.

item.name: Create booking - totalprice as string
item.description: Sends totalprice as a string instead of a number. Documents the API's current permissive behavior.

exec: // Restful-Booker does not validate field types: sending totalprice as a string
// returns 200 and the API coerces or ignores the value.
// A stricter API should return 400 Bad Request.
pm.test("API returns 200 - observed: no type validation on totalprice", function () {

item.name: Update non-existent booking (API returns 405 - semantically should be 404)
item.description: Attempts to update booking ID 99999999. Documents the known API behavior: returns 405 instead of the semantically correct 404.

exec: // Restful-Booker returns 405 (Method Not Allowed) for non-existent booking IDs.
// The semantically correct response would be 404 (Not Found).
// This test documents the actual API behavior.
pm.test("Status code is 405 - observed API behavior for non-existent ID", function () {

item.name: Cleanup - Delete edge booking 1 (totalprice zero)
item.description: Deletes the totalprice=0 booking to avoid polluting the dataset after the edge case run.

item.name: Cleanup - Delete edge booking 2 (special characters)
item.description: Deletes the special characters booking to avoid polluting the dataset after the edge case run.
